# function: get_env

Returns the value of the environment variable `name`. If the variable is not set, the first `default` argument is returned instead; if no default is provided, the function returns an error.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Read a single environment variable inline, without a data block.
output "home" {
  value = provider::environment::get_env("HOME")
}

# Provide a default to use when the variable is not set.
output "log_level" {
  value = provider::environment::get_env("LOG_LEVEL", "info")
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
get_env(name string, default string...) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `name` (String) Name of the environment variable to read.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `default` (Variadic, String) Optional value to return when the environment variable is not set. Only the first value is used.
